preset_reason: paper GF compact BC line: native HGA-TGBC UB, Gini frontier, valid relaxation screening, compact fixed-interval MIP/BC proofs
certificate: gamma-frontier diagnostic still has relevant intervals that are neither closed nor fathomed by a valid lower bound; do not treat as an optimality certificate
incumbent_selection_reason: minimum verifier-passed route plan from seeded paper primal heuristic
incumbent_provenance: same-run native HGA-TGBC paper primal heuristic, verifier-gated UB only
notes: RunConfigSnapshot: algorithm_preset=paper-gf-compact-bc, preset_certificate_scope=gini_frontier_relaxation_then_compact_interval_branch_and_cut, pricing_engine=exact-label, final_pricing_engine=exact-label, column_tracks=elementary-only, rmp_column_space=elementary, relaxed_columns_in_rmp=false, large_instance_mode=off, station_set_backend=uint64, route_mask_all_subset_enumeration_enabled=false, incumbent_archive_auto=false, compact_fallback_enabled=false
parsed Hybrid GA text format; distances rebuilt from points at speed factor 1.5

Gamma-frontier diagnostic covers Gini intervals with fixed-Gini interval branch-price trees. It reports optimal only if every relevant interval is closed or bound-fathomed and the aggregated lower bound reaches the incumbent. Each interval also uses the valid trivial bound objective>=G>=interval_floor and a final-inventory pickup/route/Gini relaxation bound when it solves.
